points.headline: The frontier model race keeps accelerating.
points.body: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 "Sol Ultra" is reportedly heading to Codex, drawing heavy developer attention, while Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 is being framed as a comeback alongside new agent tooling. The rapid cadence of major releases shows the labs are still trading the lead on coding and agentic work.

points.headline: Anthropic's Claude hits deployment and trust friction.
points.body: Alibaba reportedly classified Claude Code as high-risk and banned employees from using it, and Claude reached general availability on Microsoft Foundry but without a European data zone, leaving EU banking and healthcare unable to deploy it in production. Developers meanwhile report Fable 5 shipping real production code (a roughly $149 sqlite-utils release), even as Opus 4.8 sometimes invents fields in tool calls.

points.headline: Humanoid robotics pushes toward commercialization.
points.body: Agility Robotics is going public via SPAC while its CEO tempers promises of a home robot anytime soon, and startup Humanoid claims its KinetIQ Ascend reinforcement-learning approach reaches 99.9% manipulation reliability at human speed. RoboCup 2026 in Incheon, South Korea capped the week with its humanoid-league finals.

points.headline: AI tutoring shows measurable classroom gains.
points.body: Wealthy US families are turning to AI-driven schools like Alpha and Forge Prep instead of traditional classrooms, and a new AI tutor posted a 0.71 to 1.30 standard-deviation effect size in a Dartmouth course. The results strengthen the case that personalized AI instruction can outperform conventional teaching, at least for some students.

points.headline: Agent payments and enterprise MCP move to the edge.
points.body: Cloudflare and AWS both implemented x402 stablecoin micropayments for agent-to-service transactions, reviving HTTP 402 with sub-cent costs and 169 million transactions in year one. The Model Context Protocol also promoted its enterprise-managed authorization extension to stable, letting organizations control agent access through their existing identity provider.

points.headline: The AI ROI reckoning arrives.
points.body: Enterprise buyers increasingly find deployed AI falls short of the demo six to twelve months in, and analyses warn that per-task AI spend could exceed the cost of a human engineer within a few years. Firms like Trunk Tools are responding by ditching general-purpose models for specialized stacks, cutting document review from 60 days to 10.
